deleteSafeNote: 406 sem4 ClassGroups have zero downstream refs (technical observation only). NOT a recommendation to delete all. Controlled cleanup requires backup, confirm token, transaction, preserve canonical 227, remove verified extras, post-audit. See l8-c0a addendum.

schemaAudit.notes: ClassGroup is currently semester-scoped. @@unique([semesterId, name]) prevents global uniqueness. Making global requires: (1) make semesterId nullable, (2) change unique to [name] only, (3) migrate existing data, (4) update all API/UI/import/scheduler queries.

IMPORT.description: Import pipeline creates ClassGroup via upsert with semesterId. confirmImportBatch() in importer.ts uses semesterId to scope ClassGroup lookup/creation. Making ClassGroup global would require removing semesterId from upsert conditions.

COURSE_SETTING.description: Course setting preview queries ClassGroup by targetSemesterId. If ClassGroup becomes global, query should drop semesterId filter.

SCHEDULE_DISPLAY.description: Schedule grid displays ClassGroup names via TeachingTaskClass. No direct semesterId filter on ClassGroup in display, but indirect via TeachingTask.semesterId.

SCHEDULER_SOLVER.description: Solver loads ClassGroup via TeachingTaskClass. Does not filter ClassGroup by semesterId directly.

ADJUSTMENT.description: Adjustment system references ClassGroup indirectly via TeachingTaskClass. No direct ClassGroup semesterId query.

ADMIN_DB_PAGE.description: Admin DB page queries ClassGroup by active semester. Making ClassGroup global would require removing semester filter.

SETTINGS.description: Settings center does not directly manage ClassGroup.

SEMESTER_SCOPING.description: ClassGroup.semesterId is the core of semester scoping for class groups. Making global requires schema migration + all query updates.

API_VALIDATION.description: API routes validate ClassGroup existence within semester scope. Global ClassGroup would need different validation.

recommendedOption: HYBRID (Option A short-term cleanup + Option B long-term globalization)
